preamble: The shift from the voiceless alveolar affricate [ts] to the voiced alveolar affricate [dz] is a standard form of voicing lenition. It typically occurs in intervocalic positions where the vocal folds begin vibrating during the affricate production.

examples.note: Regional variation where intervocalic affricates are voiced.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
